post_topic: Why most 'multi-agent' marketing systems are one fat prompt — and what a real dispatch/verdict swarm actually requires
image_description:
Single standalone diagram. One concept only. No panels, no grids, no collages. Full bleed 1080x1080px white background.

A left-to-right technical architecture flow diagram titled 'Dispatch / Verdict Swarm Architecture' in bold navy #0A0F1E at the top.

Far left: a rounded rectangle box labeled 'Task Input' in navy. An arrow points right from Task Input into a central hexagon labeled 'Dispatcher' in navy with a blue #5a96ff outline. Below the Dispatcher hexagon, a small label reads 'classifies + routes by context'.

From the Dispatcher hexagon, three arrows fan out to the right toward three separate rounded rectangle boxes arranged vertically, labeled respectively: 'Copy Agent', 'Research Agent', and 'Brand Validation Agent'. Each box is outlined in blue #5a96ff with navy text. Each box has a small subtitle beneath it: 'writes output', 'pulls context', and 'checks rules' respectively.

All three agent boxes point with arrows to a single box to their right labeled 'Verdict Layer' in navy with an amber #F5A623 thick border. Inside the Verdict Layer box, a smaller line of text reads 'Scores output against MRR floor / HITL gate'. An amber callout badge on the top-right corner of the Verdict Layer box reads 'HITL lives here'.

From the Verdict Layer box, two arrows exit: one arrow points right to a rounded rectangle labeled 'Output ✓' outlined in blue; a second arrow curves back left and downward, looping back toward the Dispatcher, labeled 'Revise' in amber #F5A623.

Below the entire flow, a single horizontal divider line separates the diagram from a two-column contrast note. Left column header in amber: 'No Floor' with body text 'Optimizes for throughput'. Right column header in blue: 'Floor Enforced' with body text 'Optimizes for quality per unit'.

Navy #0A0F1E primary elements, blue #5a96ff highlights, amber #F5A623 callouts and important labels. Clean sans-serif font. Real cloud provider logos where relevant. Small text 'Kelvin Davis' bottom right corner. Professional LinkedIn infographic style similar to ByteByteGo. White background. No dark backgrounds.
